name: Oriental Noodles with Chili and Cashews
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Beat the eggs (keep 4 and 1 separate)
Finely chop one of the chiles, and the spring onion.
Thinly slice the snow peas. Finely shred the lime rind
and second red chili.
As an alternative to making your own noodles, Chinese
thread noodles or tagliatelle are suitable substitutes.
1. Sift flour and salt into a bowl. Make a well in the center and add 4 beaten eggs. Gradually incorporate into the flour with a fork. Turn onto lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes). Cover and stand for 30 minutes.
2. Divide dough into 4 portions. Roll one portion to 3mm thickness using a rolling pin or sharp knife. Cut into 5mm wide strips. Unroll the strands carefully. Repeat with remaining dough. Spread onto dry tea towel and sprinkle with plain flour. Set aside.
3. Prepare sauce while pasta is standing. Heat oil in a pan and add chili, spring onion, and snow peas. Cook over low heat for 2-3 minutes. Add sesame oil, soy sauce, vinegar, cashew paste, lime juice, and rind. Stir to combine. Cook, stirring over gentle heat for 3-4 minutes.
4. Cook pasta in a large pan of boiling water until just tender. Drain, spoon over sauce, and toss to combine. Sprinkle shredded chili, cashews, and serve.
